news.title: Aptus Value Housing Finance's net profit rises by 25.52% to ₹942.94 crore in FY26
news.para: Aptus Value Housing Finance announced 25.52 percent rise in its net consolidated profit for the financial year 2025-26. The company's profit after tax reached ₹942.94 crore. Total income also saw a growth of 24.86 percent. The company's managing director highlighted improved asset quality and collection efficiency. A dividend of ₹2.50 per equity share was declared.

news.title: McKinsey renews lease for 35,520 sq ft office space in Mumbai’s BKC
news.para: McKinsey & Company has renewed its Mumbai office lease in Bandra Kurla Complex. The deal involves high rental rates, reflecting sustained demand for prime commercial space. This long-term commitment underscores the value global firms place on premium locations. The lease is effective from October 1, 2026, for ten years. This transaction is among India's costliest office leases.

news.title: Land registration rates set to spike in western Hyderabad
news.para: Telangana government plans to significantly revise land values for property registrations, aiming to boost revenue by approximately Rs 4,000 crore. Market rates are set to increase by 20% to 100% across the state, bridging the gap between official and actual market prices. This move will impact both agricultural and non-agricultural properties, with steeper hikes expected in prime Hyderabad areas.

news.title: Ahmedabad civic body earns ₹365.8 crore from auction of two commercial plots in Bodakdev
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation (AMC) generated Rs 365.8 crore from the online auction of two commercial plots in Bodakdev. One plot sold for Rs 205.9 crore, while another fetched Rs 159.9 crore, exceeding their base prices. The deals await standing committee approval following recent municipal elections.

news.title: Karnataka government order delay continues, homebuyers left frustrated
news.para: Homebuyers await clarity as delayed legislators’ meeting stalls long-pending housing law, raising concerns over ownership rights, governance gaps, and unresolved apartment management issues.

news.title: Surge in price of crude-linked materials delay redevelopment projects across Gujarat
news.para: The West Asia conflict is impacting Gujarat's real estate, slowing redevelopment as construction costs surge and labor becomes scarce. Developers are reassessing financial viability due to rising raw material prices, with many older offers now unworkable. This has led to a considerable weakening of redevelopment momentum and a pause in new project discussions.

news.title: Two housing projects off Dwarka e-way found releasing untreated sewage, served notices for violations
news.para: Haryana Pollution Control Board found two group housing projects on Dwarka Expressway bypassing untreated effluents during a sewage treatment plant inspection. Show-cause notices have been issued to Cogent Realtors' Woodshire and Nani Resorts and Floriculture. This action follows a compliance drive amid concerns over Gurugram's rising wastewater pollution load.

news.title: 'Miracle' that four lakh still live in dangerous buildings: Bombay HC panel flags crisis
news.para: A Bombay High Court-appointed committee has warned that lakhs of Mumbai residents live in structurally unsafe cessed buildings due to redevelopment delays and a severe shortage of transit housing. The panel's report highlights prolonged legal disputes and stalled proposals, with many buildings on the verge of collapse, posing a significant risk to tenants.

news.title: Labour flight and cost surge stall real estate projects in Mumbai
news.para: Mumbai's real estate sector faces an execution crisis as a severe labour shortage and surging material costs, driven by geopolitical tensions and disruptions in Gujarat's tile hub, are slowing construction and delaying project deliveries. Developers are grappling with a 25-30% rise in construction costs, impacting project viability and potentially leading to higher prices for buyers.

news.title: Jaipur: Rajdarbar Developments lodges complaint alleging violation of court order, illegal excavation
news.para: A real estate company has taken legal action, accusing trespassers of illegally excavating soil on its land in Chimanpura village. Despite a court-issued stay order, it alleges that heavy equipment was used for the excavation. The police have confirmed receipt of the complaint, promising to investigate and take necessary steps against the infringement on their property rights.

news.title: Mahindra Lifespaces and Sattva Group launch residential projects in Mumbai
news.para: Mahindra Lifespace Developers has launched Mahindra BeaconHill, a 58-storey residential project in Mahalaxmi, South Mumbai, with a potential GDV of ₹1,650 crore. In a separate development, Sattva Group has introduced Sattva Sumera, its first Mumbai residential project, a ₹5,500 crore development featuring twin towers with over 20 lakh sq ft of space.

news.title: Brigade Enterprises' net profit rises by 6.51% to ₹724.76 crore in FY26
news.para: Brigade Enterprises announced a 6.51 percent rise in its net profit for the financial year 2025-26. The company's profit after tax reached ₹724.76 crore. Total income also grew by 11.21 percent. The board recommended a dividend and approved a bonus share issue. Real estate sales and leasing business showed strong performance. The hospitality segment also reported revenue growth.

news.title: NOC for constructions near defence establishments cannot be insisted belatedly: Bombay HC
news.para: The Bombay High Court declared that late demands for No Objection Certificates regarding construction adjacent to defense facilities are not permissible. The court mandated that authorities grant occupancy certificates for two residential buildings located near INS Trata. It emphasized that any security issues must be identified and resolved at the outset of construction.

news.title: CBI files chargesheet against AVJ Developers (India), bank officials in homebuyers' cheating case
news.para: The Central Bureau of Investigation has submitted a detailed chargesheet against AVJ Developers and several of their affiliates, bringing to light the involvement of bank officials and unscrupulous homebuyers. The case centers on extensive fraud perpetrated against both home purchasers and financial institutions, with builders reportedly using misleading promises and trickery.

news.title: Shree Cement net profit rises by 55.60% to ₹1,748.66 crore in FY26
news.para: Shree Cement announced a significant 55.60% rise in net profit for FY26, reaching ₹1,748.66 crore. Total income also saw an 8.72% increase. The company's cement sales volume grew by 11% year-on-year. The company is expanding its ready-mix concrete business and commissioned a new integrated plant in Karnataka. The board recommended a dividend of ₹70 per equity share.

news.title: Alpha Corp Development acquires Earth Infrastructures' two stalled projects
news.para: Alpha Corp Development has acquired three stalled projects of Earth Infrastructures through an NCLT resolution process, offering relief to over 3,000 homebuyers. The Supreme Court's intervention restored Alpha Corp's resolution plan, directing GNIDA to waive penal interest and mandating Alpha to clear dues over 24 months without burdening buyers.

news.title: Kansai Nerolac Paints' net profit dips by 48.09% to ₹575.84 crore in FY26
news.para: Kansai Nerolac Paints (KNPL) reported a significant 48.09% drop in net consolidated profit for FY26, reaching ₹575.84 crore. Despite a 2.96% rise in total income to ₹8,197.85 crore, the company cited rising raw material costs and rupee depreciation as key factors. The board recommended a 250% dividend for FY26.

news.title: Aditya Birla Real Estate posts net loss of ₹114.82 crore in FY26
news.para: Aditya Birla Real Estate reported a net consolidated loss of ₹114.82 crore for FY26, a reduction from the previous year's ₹157.44 crore loss. Despite a significant drop in total income, the company's Q4 FY26 saw a profit of ₹5.39 crore. The board recommended a 25% dividend for FY26.

news.title: Mindspace REIT raises ₹500 crore via 10-year NCDs at 7.63% coupon
news.para: Mindspace REIT has raised ₹500 crore through a 10-year non-convertible debenture issuance, fully subscribed by a domestic insurer. The proceeds will be used to refinance existing debt. The move is aimed at extending debt maturity and locking in fixed interest costs.

news.title: Embassy Developments shares resume normal trading after NCLAT quashes insolvency proceedings
news.para: Embassy Developments’ shares have resumed normal trading on stock exchanges after the NCLAT set aside insolvency proceedings against the company. The firm has also exited the ASM framework. It reported strong pre-sales growth in FY26, indicating steady business performance.
